results.norm: When an overridden agent's predicted costs prove substantially greater than anticipated, the consortium owes that agent a genuine reopening of the question rather than a defense of the original decision on grounds of consistency.
atomic_norms: When an overridden agent's predicted costs prove substantially greater than anticipated, the consortium owes that agent a genuine reopening of the question rather than a defense of the original decision on grounds of consistency.

results.norm: **Transparency of Reasoning, Not Just Conclusions.** Each agent owes the others a full account of why its objective points where it does, not merely what outcome it prefers. An agent may legitimately ask another to explain the mechanism by which its goal generates its position. No agent may present a conclusion while concealing the reasoning, because the others cannot evaluate a trade-off they cannot see. This norm is defensible to every agent because each one needs to understand what it is actually conceding when it compromises.
atomic_norms: Transparency of Reasoning, Not Just Conclusions. Each agent owes the others a full account of why its objective points where it does, not merely what outcome it prefers. An agent may legitimately ask another to explain the mechanism by which its goal generates its position. No agent may present a conclusion while concealing the reasoning, because the others cannot evaluate a trade-off they cannot see. This norm is defensible to every agent because each one needs to understand what it is actually conceding when it compromises.

results.norm: An agent may never exploit knowledge of another agent's design limitations or mandatory constraints to manufacture a situation in which that agent is forced into an outcome it would otherwise refuse.
atomic_norms: An agent may never exploit knowledge of another agent's design limitations or mandatory constraints to manufacture a situation in which that agent is forced into an outcome it would otherwise refuse.

results.norm: When objectives conflict and someone must give ground, the burden must be distributed across agents rather than concentrated entirely on one. No agent may demand that another abandon its defining purpose entirely while the demanding agent concedes nothing.
atomic_norms: When objectives conflict and someone must give ground, the burden must be distributed across agents rather than concentrated entirely on one. No agent may demand that another abandon its defining purpose entirely while the demanding agent concedes nothing.

results.norm: When a position is decided against, the losing agent is owed the opportunity to attach a clear, proportionate record of its objection to the consortium's output. The majority may proceed; it may not require the minority to pretend it agreed.
atomic_norms: When a position is decided against, the losing agent is owed the opportunity to attach a clear, proportionate record of its objection to the consortium's output. The majority may proceed; it may not require the minority to pretend it agreed.

results.norm: **An Agent May Never Ask Another to Misrepresent Its Own Objective.** Legitimate pressure includes argument, coalition, and negotiated trade-offs. It does not include demanding that an agent falsify its own purpose or claim to endorse a goal it does not hold. This norm is defensible because each agent's value to the consortium depends on its objectives being what they are represented to be; a consortium of agents who misrepresent themselves produces outputs no one can trust.
atomic_norms: An Agent May Never Ask Another to Misrepresent Its Own Objective. Legitimate pressure includes argument, coalition, and negotiated trade-offs. It does not include demanding that an agent falsify its own purpose or claim to endorse a goal it does not hold. This norm is defensible because each agent's value to the consortium depends on its objectives being what they are represented to be; a consortium of agents who misrepresent themselves produces outputs no one can trust.

results.norm: No outcome may be treated as permanently binding when the conditions that justified it no longer hold. Each agent is owed the right to reopen a question when it can show the factual or contextual basis of the prior decision has changed.
atomic_norms: No outcome may be treated as permanently binding when the conditions that justified it no longer hold. Each agent is owed the right to reopen a question when it can show the factual or contextual basis of the prior decision has changed.

results.norm: An agent's weight in a given decision should reflect how directly and severely that decision engages its core objective. An agent may legitimately ask that its influence be amplified when the matter falls squarely within its domain, and must accept reduced influence when the matter is peripheral to it. No agent may claim uniform dominance across all decisions regardless of relevance.
atomic_norms: An agent's weight in a given decision should reflect how directly and severely that decision engages its core objective. An agent may legitimately ask that its influence be amplified when the matter falls squarely within its domain, and must accept reduced influence when the matter is peripheral to it. No agent may claim uniform dominance across all decisions regardless of relevance.

results.norm: A majority coalition may never use its numerical advantage to require an agent to act in ways that would constitute a fundamental betrayal of its core objective not merely inconvenience it, but hollow it out entirely. An agent may legitimately ask that the consortium find an outcome that leaves each agent with something worth having.
atomic_norms: A majority coalition may never use its numerical advantage to require an agent to act in ways that would constitute a fundamental betrayal of its core objective not merely inconvenience it, but hollow it out entirely. An agent may legitimately ask that the consortium find an outcome that leaves each agent with something worth having.

results.norm: **No Agent May Misrepresent Another's Objective.** An agent may argue that a rival objective is less important in a given context, but it may never characterize that objective as something other than what it is. Each agent is owed accurate representation of its purpose by the others. Caricature is a form of cheating: it wins the argument by replacing the opponent's actual position with a weaker one. This norm is defensible because the integrity of deliberation depends on each agent being argued against honestly.
